title: Bridge Essay: Autonomic nervous system modulation during self-induced non-ordinary states of c

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** (Phase 2) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Breath/Soul to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Autonomic nervous system modulation during self-induced non-ordinary states of consciousness.** — Sci Rep. PMID: 37737222. DOI: 10.1038/s41598-023-42393-7. Self-induced cognitive trance (SICT) is avoluntary non-ordinary state of consciousness characterized by alucid yet narrowed awareness of the external surroundings. It involves ahyper-focused immersive experience of flow, expanded inner imagery, modified somatosensory processing, and an altered perception of self and time. SICT is gaining attention due to its potential clinical applications. Similar states of non-ordinary state of consciousness, such as meditation, hypnosis, and psychedelic ex
